maintenance-status.comment: Regular releases (0.11.0 Feb 2026, 0.11.1 Mar 2026, 0.11.2 Apr 2026); last push 2026-04-19.

maturity.comment: Crate is still 0.x (0.11.2) so not technically post-1.0, but the bindings are widely used in production (many GNOME-ecosystem apps) and API changes are measured and documented across minor releases.

corporate-backing.value: Community (gtk-rs organization; several maintainers affiliated with GNOME/Igalia/Centricular)
corporate-backing.comment: No single corporate sponsor; maintained by the gtk-rs organization with contributions from GNOME ecosystem developers.

implementation.comment: Safe Rust bindings over the C GTK 4 library via gtk4-sys and gobject-introspection-generated code.

rendering-mode.comment: GTK maintains a persistent widget tree; widgets are updated on state change rather than rebuilt per frame.

reactivity-model.comment: Traditional signal/callback-based event handling with direct mutation of the retained widget tree; no framework-level signals or elm-style message passing.

thread-model.comment: GTK is not thread-safe; no gtk4 type implements Send or Sync. Work on other threads must communicate via channels or glib's main context.

renderer.comment: GSK provides NGL (OpenGL), Vulkan, and Cairo (software) backends. NGL is the default since 4.14; Vulkan is selectable via GSK_RENDERER. Broadway renderer streams to a browser but is a separate backend.

gpu-acceleration.comment: Default GSK renderer is GPU-accelerated (NGL/OpenGL); Vulkan available as an opt-in.

vector-rendering.comment: GTK draws via GSK scene nodes and Cairo; resolution-independent 2D primitives available. SVG icon rendering uses a built-in parser with librsvg fallback (since 4.20).

custom-shaders.comment: GskGLShader was deprecated and no cross-renderer custom-shader API exists. Custom GPU rendering is done by embedding a GtkGLArea viewport rather than injecting shaders into GSK.

embedded-custom-rendering.comment: GtkGLArea embeds a custom OpenGL viewport; gtk4-rs ships glium_gl_area and femtovg_area examples demonstrating embedded custom rendering.

linux-wayland.comment: Native Wayland support via gdk4-wayland; GTK 4.20 improved Wayland protocol coverage (wl_seat v10, tablet v2).

android.comment: GTK 4.18 introduced an experimental Android backend, but it is not production-ready and gtk4-rs build-system support for Android has open issues.

web-wasm.comment: The Broadway GDK backend streams a GTK app to a browser via a server process, but GTK itself does not compile to WebAssembly.

embedded-no-std.comment: Requires libc, glib, and the GTK C library; not usable on no_std targets.

authoring-style.comment: Imperative builder-style Rust API is primary; GtkBuilder .ui XML (incl. composite templates via the gtk4-macros crate) is supported for declarative UI markup.

hot-reload.comment: No built-in hot reload; code and template changes require recompile. The GTK Inspector allows live CSS/property tweaks but is not a development hot-reload pipeline.

layout-system.comment: Layout managers include GtkBoxLayout (auto), GtkGridLayout (grid), GtkConstraintLayout (constraints), GtkFixedLayout (manual coords), plus custom layout managers.

styling-approach.comment: GTK has its own CSS-like stylesheet language with selectors, pseudo-classes, and a subset of CSS properties.

theme-switching.comment: Runtime theme/dark-mode switching via GtkSettings and the gtk-application-prefer-dark-theme property; libadwaita-based apps follow the system color scheme automatically.

accessibility.comment: at-spi backend on Linux (native) is default; AccessKit backend merged in GTK 4.18 enables accessibility on Windows and macOS for the first time.

internationalization.comment: gettext-based i18n; Pango handles complex scripts, RTL, and bidirectional text rendering.

widget-breadth.comment: Rich stdlib including ColumnView (tables), TreeListModel/TreeExpander (trees), TextView (rich text), GtkFileDialog, popovers, dialogs, assistants, and hundreds of widget classes covering typical desktop-app needs.

data-grid-table.comment: GtkColumnView is the modern virtualized tabular widget; GtkTreeView (now deprecated) remains available.

rich-text-markdown.comment: GtkTextView with GtkTextBuffer/GtkTextTag supports full rich-text rendering; no first-party markdown widget, but third-party crates (e.g., marker-parser) integrate with TextView.

tree-outline.comment: Hierarchical lists via GtkTreeListModel + GtkTreeExpander + GtkListView; legacy GtkTreeView also available.

native-menus.comment: GtkPopoverMenu and GMenu provide menu bars and context menus; on macOS, the menu bar is bridged to the native app menu where applicable.

file-dialogs.comment: GtkFileDialog (since 4.10) is the modern file picker; uses native portal dialogs under Flatpak/Wayland.

charts-plotting.comment: No first-party charting widget; third-party crates (e.g., plotters with a GTK4 backend) are commonly used.

custom-widgets.comment: Well-documented GObject subclassing pattern via the glib::wrapper! and glib::Object machinery, but authoring a custom widget still requires understanding GObject semantics (ObjectSubclass, properties, signals) and writing moderate boilerplate.

async-runtime-compatible.comment: GLib's main context is a native futures executor (spawn_future_local). smol/async-std integrate directly. Tokio requires running a separate tokio runtime on another thread and communicating via async channels.

system-tray.comment: GtkStatusIcon is deprecated (since GTK 3.14) and not available in GTK 4. Tray-icon support requires third-party crates like ksni or libappindicator bindings.

notifications.comment: GNotification via GApplication; desktop-integrated (freedesktop, macOS, Windows) when the app is properly registered.

clipboard.comment: GdkClipboard provides built-in clipboard read/write with a ContentProvider abstraction.

drag-and-drop.comment: GTK 4 has a rewritten DnD API using GtkDragSource, GtkDropTarget, and ContentProvider.

embedded-webview.comment: Not built in, but the separate webkit6-rs (WebKitGTK) crate provides an embeddable WebView widget for GTK 4.

multi-window.comment: GtkApplication manages multiple top-level GtkWindow instances.

binary-size.comment: Not measured in this pass. On Linux with GTK installed system-wide, a release binary is typically small-to-moderate because gtk/glib are linked dynamically; bundling the GTK runtime on Windows or macOS pushes distributions into the large category.

idle-redraw.comment: Retained-mode: GTK only redraws regions invalidated by state/property changes or explicit queue_draw calls.

dependency-count.comment: Approximate: pulling gtk4 transitively brings in gtk4-sys, gdk4, gsk4, glib, gio, cairo-rs, pango, graphene-rs, gdk-pixbuf, and their -sys crates plus system-deps — on the order of 50-100 crates; not measured precisely this pass.

documentation-quality.comment: Dedicated book (GUI development with Rust and GTK 4), full docs.rs API reference, migration notes, and 47 runnable examples.

api-ergonomics.comment: Builder patterns, type-safe signals, and auto-generated bindings help, but GObject semantics (RefCounted clone, glib::Object wrappers, subclassing macros, callback lifetimes) leak into user code and push ergonomics below pure-Rust alternatives.

interactive-playground.comment: No online playground; examples must be cloned and built locally.

ide-tooling.comment: No framework-specific LSP or inspector plugin. Standard rust-analyzer covers the Rust code; GtkBuilder .ui files are XML and get generic XML editor support.

dev-tools-inspector.comment: GTK Inspector (Ctrl+Shift+I / Ctrl+Shift+D or GTK_DEBUG=interactive) provides a live widget tree, property editor, CSS tweaking, and more — equivalent to browser devtools.

crates-io-downloads.comment: All-time downloads of the gtk4 crate as of 2026-04-24.

notable-production-users.value: Many GNOME-ecosystem apps — Solanum (pomodoro timer), Shortwave (internet radio), GNOME Authenticator (2FA), Health, Video Trimmer, Fractal (Matrix client), and the basis for Relm4-powered apps.

community-size.comment: Active gnome-rust Matrix channel, regular contributions from the gtk-rs organization and wider GNOME community; 200+ contributors historically across the gtk-rs project.

ecosystem-crates.comment: libadwaita-rs, webkit6-rs, gstreamer-rs, sourceview5-rs, vte4, and the full GNOME platform have first-class Rust bindings under the gtk-rs umbrella; Relm4 provides an Elm-inspired framework layer on top.
